sources.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Overarching theme on constructing mathematical arguments; maps to logical deduction and precise-symbol nodes as the point is broader than either alone.
Overarching theme on mathematical language and syntax.
Overarching theme on set-theory language and symbols applied to inequalities and probability.
Overarching theme on function definitions, domain and range.
Overarching theme on critiquing and justifying mathematical arguments and methods.
Overarching problem-solving theme on recognising mathematical structure.
Overarching theme on solving unstructured problems including in context.
Overarching theme on interpreting solutions in context; closest node is interpreting model outputs, though the point is broader than modelling alone.
Overarching theme on numerical methods permitting solutions to required accuracy; mapped to representative numerical-method nodes.
Overarching theme on evaluating accuracy and limitations of solutions including numerical methods.
Overarching theme on the mathematical problem-solving/modelling cycle.
Overarching theme on using and constructing diagrams to solve problems including mechanics.
Overarching modelling theme on abstraction and simplifying assumptions.
Overarching modelling theme on using model inputs.
Overarching modelling theme on interpreting model outputs in context.
Overarching modelling theme on refining models and evaluating appropriateness; mapped to modelling-assumptions node as the closest available.
Overarching modelling theme on understanding and using modelling assumptions.
Stage-1 proof content: structure of proof, proof by deduction and proof by exhaustion.
Stage-1 proof content: logical connectives and associated language; mapped to logical-deduction node as the closest available.
Stage-1 proof content: disproof by counter example.
Stage-2 proof content: proof by contradiction, including irrationality of root 2 and infinity of primes.
Stage-1 content: laws of indices for all rational exponents including negative and zero indices.
Stage-1 content: use and manipulation of surds including rationalising the denominator.
Stage-1 content: simultaneous equations in two variables, including one linear and one quadratic, by elimination and substitution.
Stage-1 content: quadratic functions, their graphs and discriminant conditions for real and repeated roots.
Stage-1 content: completing the square for the quadratic polynomial ax^2 + bx + c.
Stage-1 content: solving quadratic equations including equations quadratic in a function of the unknown.
Stage-1 content: solving linear and quadratic inequalities in a single variable and interpreting them graphically.
Stage-1 content: expressing solutions using 'and'/'or', set notation and interval notation.
Stage-1 content: representing linear and quadratic inequalities such as y > x + 1 and y > ax^2 + bx + c graphically.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Covers polynomial manipulation, factor theorem and simple algebraic division.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Rational-expression simplification is not separately catalogued; mapped to division and factor-theorem nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Modulus equations/inequalities and modulus graphing are both required.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: General graphs-of-functions statement including plotting versus sketching.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Sketching polynomials up to degree 4 including repeated roots and stationary points.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Reciprocal graphs and their asymptotes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Interpreting algebraic solutions through graphs.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Using intersections of different function types to solve equations.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Proportional relationships and their graphical representations.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Modulus of a linear function only.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Graphical solution of modulus equations and inequalities.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Definition of a function, domain/range, one-one/many-one and notation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Inverse and composite functions, existence condition and range restrictions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Single transformations of y=f(x).

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: No dedicated catalog node for combined transformations; mapped to individual transformation nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Partial fractions with denominators up to squared linear terms and at most three terms.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Function modelling including assumptions, limitations, refinements and comparisons.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Straight-line equations, midpoint, distance and intersection.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Parallel and perpendicular gradient conditions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Straight-line models in context including average rates of change.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Circle equation in centre-radius form.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Completing the square to find centre and radius.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Circle properties applied in coordinate geometry and intersection checks.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Parametric equations of curves and conversion to/from Cartesian form.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Parametric modelling in a variety of contexts.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Binomial expansion for positive integer n, factorial and combination notation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Link between binomial expansion and binomial probabilities.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Binomial expansion for any rational n, including approximation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Validity condition |bx/a|<1 for rational binomial expansion.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Sequences defined by nth-term formula or recurrence relation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Catalog lacks separate nodes for increasing/decreasing/periodic sequences and for sequence/series distinctions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Sigma notation for sums is not separately catalogued; mapped to the Series parent node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Arithmetic sequences and series including nth term and sum formulae.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Geometric sequences and series, finite sum and convergence/divergence.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Sum to infinity of a convergent geometric series with |r|<1.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Sequences and series in modelling contexts including growth/decay and interest problems.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Definitions of sin, cos and tan for all arguments.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Sine and cosine rules, including bearings and the ambiguous case.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Area of a triangle using 1/2 ab sin C.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Radian measure, arc length and sector area.

syllabusPoints.unmappedReason: No canonical node covers small-angle approximations for sine, cosine and tangent.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Standard small-angle approximations in radians are required but not catalogued separately.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Trigonometric graphs, symmetries, periodicities and exact values in degrees.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Exact values in radians for standard angles and multiples.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Definitions of secant, cosecant, cotangent and inverse trigonometric functions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Graphs, ranges and domains of reciprocal and inverse trigonometric functions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: tan θ = sin θ/cos θ and sin²θ + cos²θ = 1.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: 1 + tan²θ = sec²θ and 1 + cot²θ = cosec²θ.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Double-angle and addition formulae and their uses.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Geometrical proofs of addition/double-angle formulae; no dedicated catalog node, mapped to general justifying-methods node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Harmonic form a cos θ + b sin θ = R cos(θ±α) or R sin(θ±α) is not separately catalogued; mapped to addition-formulae parent.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Solving trigonometric equations in intervals, including quadratic and multiple-angle cases.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Constructing proofs involving trigonometric functions and identities.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Trigonometric functions applied to vectors, kinematics, forces and periodic contexts.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Exponential functions a^x and e^x and their graphs.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Gradient of e^(kx); formal differentiation of e^x is covered in 1.07j.
Defines log_a x as inverse of a^x; maps directly to logarithms as inverse functions.
Know and use ln x and its graph.
ln x as inverse of e^x; also links to exponential function e^x.
Laws of logarithms including product, quotient and power rules.
Solve exponential equations of form a^x = b.
Logarithmic graphs to estimate parameters in y=ax^n and y=kb^x; no exact node for log-linear/log-log reduction, mapped to closest equation/modelling nodes.
Exponential growth/decay and modelling contexts.
Derivative as gradient of tangent.
Gradient as limit of chord and rate of change; includes notation equivalence.
Sketch gradient function; curve sketching node is broader.
Second derivatives and notation equivalence; no dedicated second-derivative node.
Second derivative as rate of change of gradient; linked to concavity/inflection node.
Convex/concave sections and points of inflection using second derivative.
Differentiation from first principles for small positive integer powers.
First principles for sin x and cos x; standard differentiation node does not cover first-principles proof.
Differentiate x^n for rational n.
Differentiate e^(kx) and a^(kx).
Differentiate sin kx, cos kx, tan kx.
Derivative of ln x.
Equations of tangents and normals.
Classify stationary points as maxima or minima.
Identify increasing/decreasing functions.
Find points of inflection, including stationary and non-stationary.
Product rule and quotient rule.
Chain rule, connected rates of change and inverse functions.
Implicit and parametric differentiation for first derivative.
Construct simple differential equations in pure and applied contexts.
Fundamental theorem of calculus and indefinite/definite terminology.
Integrate x^n and find constant of integration.
Integrate e^(kx), 1/x, sin kx, cos kx and related sums.
Evaluate definite integrals.
Area between curve and x-axis including below-axis regions.
Area between two curves; includes parametric curves which are not explicitly covered by the node.
Integration as limit of a sum; mapped to definite integral concept as closest available.
Integration by substitution including inspection and finding substitutions.
Integration by parts including multiple applications and ln x.
Integrate using partial fractions with linear denominators.
First order differential equations with separable variables, general and particular solutions.
Interpret differential equation solutions in context including limitations.
Locate roots by sign change including bounds.
Understand failure of sign change methods.
Simple iterative methods with cobweb and staircase diagrams.
Newton-Raphson and recurrence relations x_{n+1}=g(x_n).
Convergence condition and failure of iterative methods.
Trapezium rule, rectangles, bounds and over/under-estimates.

syllabusPoints.unmappedReason: No canonical node covers the contextual application of numerical methods across root-finding and integration in the supplied catalog.
reviewNotes: Contextual problem solving using numerical methods; no matching node in catalog.
Vectors in two dimensions including notation and scalar/vector distinction.
Vectors in three dimensions using i, j, k notation.
Magnitude and direction of vectors; 3D direction excluded.
Vector addition diagrammatically and algebraic operations including scalar multiplication.
Position vectors and related terminology including unit vectors.
Distance between two points represented by position vectors in 2D and 3D.
Use vectors to solve problems in pure mathematics and contexts including forces; mapped to vector geometry and vector nature of force.
Vectors in kinematics; mapped to calculus in kinematics node.
Understand population and sample terminology.
Use samples to make informal inferences; no dedicated inference node in sampling section, mapped to bias/critique node as closest.
Sampling techniques including simple random and opportunity sampling.
Select or critique sampling techniques and recognise different samples lead to different conclusions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Covers interpretation of several single-variable diagrams; dot plots and vertical line charts lack dedicated nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Area-as-frequency and links to distributions; no node for advantages/disadvantages of diagrams.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Interpret scatter diagrams and regression lines; calculation of regression-line equations is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Informal interpretation of correlation; mapped to the correlation parent node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Specific correlation-versus-causation distinction is not represented by a dedicated node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Includes variance and distribution comparison; no separate variance node in the catalog.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Calculator statistical functions and grouped-data estimates lack dedicated nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Outlier recognition in data sets and diagrams; no dedicated outlier node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Selecting or critiquing presentation techniques maps only to the statistical-diagrams parent.

syllabusPoints.unmappedReason: No canonical node covers data cleaning, missing data, errors and outliers.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: No matching canonical node for data-cleaning skills.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Includes probability notation and links to distributions beyond the rule nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Appropriate diagrams for probability calculations.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Conditional probability with set notation and the addition rule for unions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Conditional probability from first principles; using the formula to find P(A|B) from P(B|A) is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Probability modelling and critiquing modelling assumptions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Simple finite discrete distributions; calculation of mean and variance is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Binomial distribution as a model.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Calculator binomial probabilities and contextual identification of conditions/assumptions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Mean and variance formulae np and npq for choosing a normal approximation to binomial.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Normal distribution as a continuous model.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Probability and inverse-normal calculations with standard normal transformation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Empirical rule and inflection points; equation of the normal curve is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Selecting an appropriate distribution with reasoning; explicit normal-approximation calculations excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Hypothesis-testing terminology developed through a binomial model.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Hypothesis test for a proportion in a binomial distribution with contextual interpretation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Significance level as probability of incorrectly rejecting H0; sample-to-population inference.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Distribution of the sample mean for normal populations; proof is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Hypothesis test for the mean of a normal distribution with known/given/assumed variance.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient as a measure of linear closeness.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Hypothesis tests using a given PMCC; calculation of correlation coefficients is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: SI base units m, s, kg not matched by a dedicated mechanics-units node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Derived units for velocity, acceleration, force and weight not matched by a dedicated node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Unit of moment; the node focuses on moments of forces rather than units.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Kinematics language and scalar/vector distinction.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Use and interpretation of kinematics graphs for straight-line motion.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Gradient/area relationships for displacement-time and velocity-time graphs.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Derivation of constant-acceleration formulae is explicitly required; proof depth assigned.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Vector extension of suvat equations using column or i,j notation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Differentiation and integration in one-dimensional kinematics.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Vector extension of calculus in kinematics.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Gravity modelled as a constant acceleration vector in a vertical plane.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Projectile model, range, greatest height and cartesian trajectory; inclined plane and resistive forces excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Vector nature of force and force diagrams in at most two dimensions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Newton's first law and completing force diagrams for equilibrium.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Newton's second law for straight-line motion of bodies under constant forces.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Newton's second law with forces expressed as two-dimensional vectors.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Resolving forces and motion on inclined planes in two dimensions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Weight W=mg and straight-line motion under gravity.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Gravitational acceleration g and its value in SI units; inverse-square law is excluded.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Newton's third law maps directly to the dedicated node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Normal reaction force has a direct matching node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: No exact node for the smooth-contact model; mapped to the general modelling-assumptions node as a specific instance.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Connected particles and smooth pulleys map directly to the connected-particles node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Combines Newton's third law with resolving forces in two dimensions.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Equilibrium condition on resolved components maps directly to the principle-of-equilibrium node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: 2-D vector equilibrium problems combine resolving forces with vector notation.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Advanced connected-particle and pulley problems requiring resolving are a narrower application of connected particles.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Resultants and components of forces are covered by the resultants and components nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: 2-D dynamics under variable forces combines Newton's second law with vector methods.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Frictional force concept maps directly.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Contact force split into normal and frictional components maps to both relevant nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Coefficient of friction and limiting friction are covered by the two dedicated nodes.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Static/limiting equilibrium on rough surfaces combines equilibrium principles with limiting friction.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Motion on a rough surface maps directly to the dynamics-with-friction node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Moment of a force has a direct node; note the catalog path is labelled Further Mechanics but the content is in the standard A-Level specification.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Rigid-body equilibrium conditions map directly to the rigid-body-equilibrium node.

evidence.title: OCR A Level Mathematics A H240 specification

reviewNotes: Simple static contexts involving moments use both moments and rigid-body-equilibrium nodes.
